messages.text: Nacu, whats the summary and conclusions to be drawn from the attached presentation?

• 45% RAP with Vitala performs better than 40% without a rejuvenator, the results show that marginally better than the competitor (Relixer), however the contractor used more virgin asphalt in the Relixer mix which will alter the results.  
• 50% RAP with Vitala performs marginally better than the competitor rejuvenator but worst than the 40% without rejuvenator. 
• Conclusions:  1. all results for both rejuvenators are basically the same,  2. These results will help us be added to the list of allowed rejuvenators by NY DOT. 3. the price of Relixer  approx. (october 2023) was  between $1.10-$1.20/ lb. 4. If we want to compete we will need to be cost competitive and most importantly have excellent customer service, as when I was with the customer it was something that they mentioned to me several times.

Depends on the dosage rate. Would have been a good idea to have the asphalt mix expert involved in this project. We have shown repeatedly in lab and field studies we performed. The fact that the 50% RAP with Vitala performs worse than 40% RAP mix makes no sense at all.

Ok, understand...they need customer service or technical support? 

Any competitive info on Relixer?
Can you provide the details of the results beyond the summary?
Its the same dosage. Nam and rutgers was involved. NYC control the parameters of the project

Understand, our friend at Rutgers has a degrees in geotechnical engineering not materials nor asphalt materials. I also think we have a lot more knowledge about our materials and putting high performing mixes together.
Why would we use the same dosage at a higher RAP?

NYC decided the additive dosage.
NYC & city asphalt did not want the additive companies involved as they got in trouble in the past for involving Cargill in projects.
The same dosage between Vitala and Relixer at the 45% and 50%, the 50% had more rejuvenator added.
Do we have the detailed results beyond the summary?
Did we obtain any samples from the project?
No, the report is not done yet, since they haven't finished all the tests. We don't have samples for the project as they were sent to the water tests.
We ddin't obtain samples, as NYDOT didn't allow us to obtain samples as they wanted to control the flow of information.

Irena Nedeljkovic Specifically stated to me and Karen Edwards that NYC DOT will control every aspect of the project including results and publicity. Iowa State concluded that testing will be oversaw by Nam at NCAT and Rugters.
